exercises.title: gRPC security error codes
exercises.instructions:
Match each scenario to the correct gRPC Status code:
A: Client sends no Authorization header
B: Client sends valid JWT but tries to access admin endpoint without ADMIN role
C: Client sends expired JWT token
D: gRPC server TLS certificate not trusted by client
E: Client sends valid JWT and has correct permissions
exercises.expected_solution:
A: Status.UNAUTHENTICATED
   No credentials provided at all
   gRPC standard: missing authentication = UNAUTHENTICATED
   HTTP equivalent: 401 Unauthorized
   Message: 'Authorization header is required'

B: Status.PERMISSION_DENIED
   Valid identity, insufficient permissions
   Client authenticated (we know WHO they are)
   But they lack the required role/scope
   HTTP equivalent: 403 Forbidden
   Message: 'ADMIN role required for this operation'

C: Status.UNAUTHENTICATED
   Expired token = invalid authentication
   Token can't be trusted → treat as no valid credentials
   HTTP equivalent: 401 Unauthorized
   Message: 'Token has expired, please re-authenticate'
   Note: NOT PERMISSION_DENIED — the identity itself can't be verified

D: Status.UNAVAILABLE (or connection error)
   TLS handshake failure happens at transport layer
   Client never reaches the RPC layer — connection refused
   Not a gRPC status code per se — channel fails to establish
   Fix: add server's CA cert to client trust store

E: Status.OK (implicit — no status set)
   Success path: no explicit status needed
   RPC completes, response sent normally
   HTTP equivalent: 200 OK

test_cases.expected:
A: Status.UNAUTHENTICATED
   No credentials provided at all
   gRPC standard: missing authentication = UNAUTHENTICATED
   HTTP equivalent: 401 Unauthorized
   Message: 'Authorization header is required'

B: Status.PERMISSION_DENIED
   Valid identity, insufficient permissions
   Client authenticated (we know WHO they are)
   But they lack the required role/scope
   HTTP equivalent: 403 Forbidden
   Message: 'ADMIN role required for this operation'

C: Status.UNAUTHENTICATED
   Expired token = invalid authentication
   Token can't be trusted → treat as no valid credentials
   HTTP equivalent: 401 Unauthorized
   Message: 'Token has expired, please re-authenticate'
   Note: NOT PERMISSION_DENIED — the identity itself can't be verified

D: Status.UNAVAILABLE (or connection error)
   TLS handshake failure happens at transport layer
   Client never reaches the RPC layer — connection refused
   Not a gRPC status code per se — channel fails to establish
   Fix: add server's CA cert to client trust store

E: Status.OK (implicit — no status set)
   Success path: no explicit status needed
   RPC completes, response sent normally
   HTTP equivalent: 200 OK

hints: UNAUTHENTICATED: missing or invalid credentials (can't verify identity)
PERMISSION_DENIED: identity verified but insufficient access
TLS failure: transport-level, not an RPC status

exercises.title: Complete the gRPC auth interceptor
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks in the gRPC server-side auth interceptor.

blanks.hint: pass to next handler in the chain

hints: UNAUTHENTICATED: missing OR invalid credentials
startCall: forward to actual handler after auth passes
Return empty Listener after close: prevents further processing

exercises.title: Token extraction from gRPC Metadata
exercises.instructions:
Write a TokenExtractor utility:
1. Extract 'authorization' header from gRPC Metadata
2. Validate Bearer format
3. Return the raw token string
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): valid bearer, missing header, wrong format
hints: Metadata.Key.of('authorization'): lowercase key name (gRPC canonicalization)
Bearer: prefix is exactly 7 chars including space
StatusRuntimeException: throw this to abort the RPC with a specific status

exercises.title: gRPC server interceptor for JWT validation
exercises.instructions:
Write JwtServerInterceptor:
1. Implements ServerInterceptor
2. Extract 'authorization' from Metadata
3. Validate JWT (mock validator for test)
4. Missing/invalid → Status.UNAUTHENTICATED
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): valid token passes, invalid blocked
hints: Empty Listener: return after call.close() to prevent further processing
Set<String> validTokens: production = JwtDecoder.decode(token)
@GrpcGlobalServerInterceptor: applied to all services automatically

exercises.title: Role-based authorization in gRPC interceptor
exercises.instructions:
Add role-based authorization:
1. Extend interceptor to check merchant vs admin role
2. Extract role from JWT claims (simulated: token prefix = role)
3. AdminOnly annotation-like: check if method requires ADMIN
4. Valid token + wrong role → Status.PERMISSION_DENIED
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): merchant can access merchant methods, admin check, PERMISSION_DENIED
hints: UNAUTHENTICATED: can't verify identity; PERMISSION_DENIED: identity known, access denied
fullMethodName: 'ServiceName/MethodName' format in gRPC
Role.valueOf(): parse role from token prefix

exercises.title: gRPC client credentials setup
exercises.instructions:
Write gRPC client with authentication:
1. Build ManagedChannel with call credentials
2. CallCredentials: attach 'authorization' header per call
3. Per-RPC credentials vs channel credentials
4. @SpringBootTest: credentials builder produces correct metadata
hints: MetadataUtils.newAttachHeadersInterceptor: simplest way to add headers per call
CallCredentials: more robust — called per RPC, supports async token refresh
Metadata key lowercase: gRPC HTTP/2 headers are lowercase

exercises.title: TLS configuration for gRPC
exercises.instructions:
Write gRPC TLS configuration:
1. Server: application.yml with certificate chain + private key paths
2. Client: TlsChannelCredentials with CA certificate trust
3. Explain: plaintext vs TLS vs mTLS differences
4. @SpringBootTest: TLS config documented and channel credential type documented
hints: grpc.server.security.enabled=true: activates TLS in grpc-spring-boot-starter
TlsChannelCredentials.create(): standard TLS (trust system CAs)
mTLS: both client and server present certificates — strongest authentication

exercises.title: Context propagation for auth info
exercises.instructions:
Propagate authenticated identity to service methods:
1. Context.Key<AuthContext>: gRPC context storage
2. Interceptor: validate token → put AuthContext into Context
3. Service method: retrieve AuthContext from Context
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): context set by interceptor, retrieved in service
hints: Context.key('name'): creates a typed slot in gRPC's per-call context
ctx.attach()/detach(): thread-local attachment for the duration of the call
INTERNAL status: programming error (auth context missing means interceptor not configured)

exercises.title: Rate limiting interceptor
exercises.instructions:
Add rate limiting to gRPC interceptor:
1. Per-merchant rate limit: 100 RPCs per minute (Bucket4j)
2. Extract merchantId from Metadata
3. Bucket exhausted → Status.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): within limit passes, exceeded blocks
hints: Status.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED: gRPC's 429 equivalent
x-merchant-id: extracted from Metadata (not from auth token)
Bucket4j per-merchant: same pattern as HTTP rate limiting

exercises.title: Security interceptor order analysis
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay has 4 gRPC interceptors registered. They run in this order: RateLimiting → Auth → RBAC → Logging.

For each request scenario, trace which interceptors run and what happens:
A: No auth header, rate limit available
B: Valid token, MERCHANT role, accessing MERCHANT-level endpoint
C: Valid token, MERCHANT role, accessing ADMIN-only endpoint
D: Rate limit exhausted (bucket empty), valid token present
E: Valid token, ADMIN role, accessing ADMIN endpoint
exercises.expected_solution:
A: No auth header, rate limit available
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES (limit not exceeded, token consumed)
   Auth: ✗ STOPS — UNAUTHENTICATED (no Bearer header)
   RBAC: ✗ NOT REACHED (Auth closed the call)
   Logging: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Response: Status.UNAUTHENTICATED, 'Bearer token required'
   Note: 1 rate limit token consumed even though auth failed!
   Consider: check auth BEFORE rate limit to avoid wasting tokens

B: Valid token, MERCHANT, MERCHANT endpoint
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES
   Auth: ✓ PASSES (valid token)
   RBAC: ✓ PASSES (MERCHANT can access MERCHANT endpoint)
   Logging: ✓ PASSES (logs success)
   Response: Status.OK, payment processed

C: Valid token, MERCHANT, ADMIN endpoint
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES
   Auth: ✓ PASSES
   RBAC: ✗ STOPS — PERMISSION_DENIED (MERCHANT cannot access ADMIN endpoint)
   Logging: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Response: Status.PERMISSION_DENIED, 'ADMIN role required'
   Note: 1 rate limit token consumed

D: Rate limit exhausted, valid token present
   RateLimiting: ✗ STOPS — RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (bucket empty)
   Auth: ✗ NOT REACHED
   RBAC: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Logging: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Response: Status.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, 'Rate limit exceeded'
   Note: Fast fail — no JWT parsing needed, efficient!

E: Valid token, ADMIN, ADMIN endpoint
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES
   Auth: ✓ PASSES
   RBAC: ✓ PASSES (ADMIN can access ADMIN endpoint)
   Logging: ✓ PASSES
   Response: Status.OK

INSIGHT on interceptor order:
  Rate limit FIRST: fast fail, no expensive JWT parsing if exhausted
  Auth BEFORE RBAC: can't check roles without verified identity
  Logging LAST: logs the final outcome (success or which stage failed)

test_cases.expected:
A: No auth header, rate limit available
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES (limit not exceeded, token consumed)
   Auth: ✗ STOPS — UNAUTHENTICATED (no Bearer header)
   RBAC: ✗ NOT REACHED (Auth closed the call)
   Logging: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Response: Status.UNAUTHENTICATED, 'Bearer token required'
   Note: 1 rate limit token consumed even though auth failed!
   Consider: check auth BEFORE rate limit to avoid wasting tokens

B: Valid token, MERCHANT, MERCHANT endpoint
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES
   Auth: ✓ PASSES (valid token)
   RBAC: ✓ PASSES (MERCHANT can access MERCHANT endpoint)
   Logging: ✓ PASSES (logs success)
   Response: Status.OK, payment processed

C: Valid token, MERCHANT, ADMIN endpoint
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES
   Auth: ✓ PASSES
   RBAC: ✗ STOPS — PERMISSION_DENIED (MERCHANT cannot access ADMIN endpoint)
   Logging: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Response: Status.PERMISSION_DENIED, 'ADMIN role required'
   Note: 1 rate limit token consumed

D: Rate limit exhausted, valid token present
   RateLimiting: ✗ STOPS — RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (bucket empty)
   Auth: ✗ NOT REACHED
   RBAC: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Logging: ✗ NOT REACHED
   Response: Status.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, 'Rate limit exceeded'
   Note: Fast fail — no JWT parsing needed, efficient!

E: Valid token, ADMIN, ADMIN endpoint
   RateLimiting: ✓ PASSES
   Auth: ✓ PASSES
   RBAC: ✓ PASSES (ADMIN can access ADMIN endpoint)
   Logging: ✓ PASSES
   Response: Status.OK

INSIGHT on interceptor order:
  Rate limit FIRST: fast fail, no expensive JWT parsing if exhausted
  Auth BEFORE RBAC: can't check roles without verified identity
  Logging LAST: logs the final outcome (success or which stage failed)

hints: Interceptors run in order: early failure prevents downstream processing
Rate limit before auth: avoids JWT parsing when exhausted
Logging last: captures the final outcome

exercises.title: Complete auth interceptor stack
exercises.instructions:
Build complete auth stack:
1. RateLimitingInterceptor: 100 RPCs/min per merchantId
2. AuthInterceptor: validate Bearer token → set AuthContext in Context
3. RbacInterceptor: check role vs admin-only methods
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): rate limit, auth required, RBAC, full happy path
hints: exhaust(): consume all tokens so next request is blocked
Token format 'ROLE:merchantId': parse by splitting on ':'
Happy path: all 3 checks pass in sequence

exercises.title: Audit logging interceptor
exercises.instructions:
Write a gRPC audit logging interceptor:
1. Log every RPC: method name, merchantId, status code, latencyMs
2. Log BEFORE forwarding (timestamp) and AFTER completion (status)
3. AuditLog entity + repository
4. @DataJpaTest: successful call logged, failed call logged with error status
hints: Audit log: required for RBI compliance on financial gRPC APIs
success=statusCode.equals('OK'): simple success detection
latencyMs: measure from interceptCall start to call.close()

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay gRPC security system
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Razorpay gRPC security:
1. Token validator: validate Bearer token, extract merchantId + role
2. Rate limiter: 100 RPCs/min per merchantId
3. RBAC checker: admin-only methods
4. Audit logger: log every call
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): valid flow, invalid token, rate limit, RBAC block
hints: GrpcCallContext: carries merchantId+role through the interceptor chain
exhaust(): drain all 100 tokens before testing the block
audit.getTotal()>0: just verify it was called

exercises.title: mTLS service-to-service authentication
exercises.instructions:
Document and implement mTLS for Razorpay internal services:
1. ServiceIdentity record: serviceName, pod, namespace (from TLS cert CN/SAN)
2. ServiceAuthValidator: accept calls only from known internal services
3. Service allowlist: payment-service, fraud-service, settlement-service
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): allowed service passes, unknown service blocked, malformed identity rejected
hints: mTLS: certificate = identity, no separate JWT needed
CN format 'service.pod.namespace': k8s-style naming
PERMISSION_DENIED for unknown service (identity verified, not authorized)

exercises.title: Complete M28 T4 gRPC Security synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M28 T4 synthesis:
1. TokenValidator: Bearer token → CallContext (merchantId, role)
2. RateLimiter: 100 RPCs/min per merchant
3. RbacChecker: admin-only method set
4. AuditCounter: count total + failed calls
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): happy path, invalid token, rate limit, RBAC
6. All 4 pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M28 T4 gRPC Security synthesis
hints: Limit=5 for test: faster to exhaust than 100
drain() while loop: exhaust all tokens
PERMISSION_DENIED vs UNAUTHENTICATED: role vs identity issue
